title: dactylorhiza majalis lusus albiflora
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis lusus albiflora, also known as the pale-spotted marsh orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Dactylorhiza majalis orchid. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with the pale-spotted marsh orchid include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve circulation. Herbal preparations of the pale-spotted marsh orchid often involve drying and processing the plant's roots and leaves to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
